gene_region.label: LCT transcribed interval
gene_region.definition: Canonical LCT genomic interval on GRCh37 / hg19 from Ensembl GRCh37 lookup, aligned to the hg19 coordinate system used by this app.

promoter_review_region.label: Operational promoter review window
promoter_review_region.definition: A practical 1 kb upstream window relative to the reverse-strand transcription start, used to flag promoter-adjacent coverage before the canonical gene interval.

promoter_hotspot_region.label: Promoter-associated CpG review window
promoter_hotspot_region.definition: Promoter-proximal CpG-focused span represented by the bundled EPIC subset and used here as the main methylation-review hotspot.

gene_context.gene_summary: LCT encodes lactase-phlorizin hydrolase, an intestinal brush-border enzyme that digests lactose and whose adult persistence is shaped by upstream regulatory variation.
gene_context.clinical_context: The local LCT bundle is nutrition-genetics and regulatory-enhancer oriented. Lactase persistence and intolerance interpretation depends on ancestry, genotype, age, diet, symptoms, and non-genetic gastrointestinal context.
variant_effect_overview: rs4988235-like upstream enhancer variation is a classic lactase persistence marker in many European-ancestry contexts.
Population specificity matters because different lactase-persistence alleles occur in different ancestry groups.
condition_research_overview: Lactase persistence and lactose intolerance context
gene_context.methylation_interpretation: LCT methylation should be treated as local regulatory context around a intestinal lactose hydrolysis, brush-border disaccharidase activity, enhancer regulation, and lactase persistence biology gene. Promoter-proximal methylation can support expression-potential review, but it should not replace genotype, RNA, protein, or phenotype data.
methylation_effects: Promoter or first-exon methylation may suggest a more restrained or permissive local expression state for LCT.
Because LCT biology is tissue and pathway dependent, methylation values are context rather than a stand-alone biomarker.

Combined sequence plus methylation findings are most useful when interpreted against intestinal lactose hydrolysis, brush-border disaccharidase activity, enhancer regulation, and lactase persistence biology.
methylation_condition_research: Epigenetic regulation studies that use LCT promoter or gene-body methylation as expression-context evidence.
Variant-plus-methylation review for intestinal lactose hydrolysis, brush-border disaccharidase activity, enhancer regulation, and lactase persistence biology.
Lactase persistence and lactose intolerance context

evidence.label: Ensembl GRCh37 ENSG00000115850: LCT coordinate source

biorender_visuals.label: LCT biochemical figure starter
biorender_visuals.focus: intestinal lactose hydrolysis, brush-border disaccharidase activity, enhancer regulation, and lactase persistence biology
biorender_visuals.usage_note: BioRender template and icon-library search results are bundled as figure starters. Open the template link in BioRender, then add or replace the listed icons to make a gene-specific biochemical pathway image.

gene_context.concrete_variant_prediction: The variant observed in this sample suggests an LCT lactase-persistence thesis: the individual may carry regulatory context relevant to adult lactase expression, lactose digestion, diet-response biology, and ancestry-specific enhancer evolution. Symptom interpretation still depends on diet, age, microbiome, and gastrointestinal context.

variant_records.clinical_interpretation: rs4988235 (-13910 C>T lactase persistence marker) is curated as a LCT marker for Lactase persistence and lactose intolerance context, Intestinal brush-border carbohydrate digestion. Read it through genotype dosage, phenotype context, and the intestinal lactose hydrolysis, brush-border disaccharidase activity, enhancer regulation, and lactase persistence biology pathway rather than as a stand-alone deterministic result.
variant_records.clinical_significance: Curated research and clinical-context marker; external clinical classification is required for diagnostic use.
functional_effects: Can modify or tag intestinal lactose hydrolysis, brush-border disaccharidase activity, enhancer regulation, and lactase persistence biology.
Best interpreted with tissue, ancestry, phenotype, and assay context.
associated_conditions: Lactase persistence and lactose intolerance context
research_context: Use this marker as a LCT pathway-context signal, not as a stand-alone diagnosis.
Sequence, methylation, expression, and protein data may point in different directions and should be reconciled.
variant_records.methylation_interpretation: Pair rs4988235 (-13910 C>T lactase persistence marker) with LCT methylation only as local regulatory context; methylation does not prove the variant's biochemical effect.

literature_findings.genotypes: rs4988235 (-13910 C>T lactase persistence marker) or linked LCT locus genotype states
literature_findings.phenotype: Lactase persistence and lactose intolerance context, Intestinal brush-border carbohydrate digestion
literature_findings.finding: The local curation treats rs4988235 (-13910 C>T lactase persistence marker) as a gene-specific marker for Lactase persistence and lactose intolerance context, Intestinal brush-border carbohydrate digestion and intestinal lactose hydrolysis, brush-border disaccharidase activity, enhancer regulation, and lactase persistence biology.
